2603.20965.title: Learning to Aggregate Zero-Shot LLM Agents for Corporate Disclosure Classification
2603.20965.paper_summary: This paper studies whether a lightweight trained aggregator can combine diverse zero-shot large language model judgments into a stronger downstream signal for corporate disclosure classification. Zero-shot LLMs can read disclosures without task-specific fine-tuning, but their predictions often vary across prompts, reasoning styles, and model families. I address this problem with a multi-agent framework in which three zero-shot agents independently read each disclosure and output a sentiment label, a confidence score, and a short rationale. A logistic meta-classifier then aggregates these signals to predict next-day stock return direction. I use a sample of 18,420 U.S. corporate disclosures issued by Nasdaq and S&P 500 firms between 2018 and 2024, matched to next-day stock returns. Results show that the trained aggregator outperforms all single agents, majority vote, confidence-weighted voting, and a FinBERT baseline. Balanced accuracy rises from 0.561 for the best single agent to 0.612 for the trained aggregator, with the largest gains in disclosures combining strong current performance with weak guidance or elevated risk. The results suggest that zero-shot LLM agents capture complementary financial signals and that supervised aggregation can turn cross-agent disagreement into a more useful classification target.
2603.20965.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文研究了一個輕量級訓練聚合器是否能將多樣的零-shot大型語言模型判斷結合成一個更強的下游信號，用於企業資訊披露分類。零-shot LLM可以在不進行特定任務微調的情況下閱讀披露，但它們的預測往往因提示、推理風格和模型家族而異。我通過一個多代理框架來解決這個問題，其中三個零-shot代理獨立閱讀每個披露並輸出情感標籤、信心分數和簡短的理由。然後，一個邏輯元分類器將這些信號聚合以預測次日的股票回報方向。我使用了2018年至2024年間納斯達克和標準普爾500公司發布的18,420份美國企業披露樣本，並與次日的股票回報匹配。結果顯示，訓練的聚合器在所有單一代理、多數投票、信心加權投票和FinBERT基準上表現更佳。平衡準確率從最佳單一代理的0.561上升至訓練聚合器的0.612，最大的增幅出現在將強勁當前表現與弱指導或升高風險結合的披露中。結果表明，零-shot LLM代理捕捉到互補的金融信號，並且監督聚合可以將跨代理的不一致轉化為更有用的分類目標。
2603.20965.author: Kemal Kirtac et.al.
